circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance:
Seif's father took Seif to his home county of Yemen for a two week visit and he never returned. 

Father worked at Weirton Steel Mill in Weirton, West Virginia. After he never returned, Seif's mom went to the Steel Mill to see if they had heard from him and they told her that he never worked there under that name. He had used an alias name to marry her. To this day, no one knows what his real name was/is. The Department of Immigration has no record of him under that name.

circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance: Triska was last seen around 23:45 in a car with an unidentified older male at the Route 88 Drive-In Restaurant in Harmony Township, just north of Ambridge, Pennsylvania on September 19, 1958. She never made it home. She had gone to a teen dance in Ambridge with friends. Becky left the dance at about 23:30. Two boys who happened to leave the dance around the same time spoke to Becky in front of the theater. She was alone, but an older man, they estimated around 35, was standing in the area and offered to give Becky a ride home. The boys commented that the man was too old to give Becky a ride and, they said, he walked away. Police arrested an Ambridge man when they learned that the car Becky was last seen in closely resembled his car. At the time, he was a parolee on a Florida morals charge. He denied knowing Becky, but refused to take a lie detector test. He was held on suspicion of a felony, while his car was sent to the Allegheny County Crime Lab. Investigators found some blood stains and strands of red hair in the car. An attempt was made to match the hair samples from the car against hair taken from Becky's hairbrush, but without the tools available today, it was never definitively proved that the hairs were Becky's. He stated that he had three nieces with red hair and the blood was his. Additionally, he had an alibi. Police learned the identity of the girl that he had a date with on the night Becky disappeared. The girl told police that they had attended a football game in Alliquippa and had parted company between 23:30 and 23:45 that night. With no body and not enough evidence, he was released. He was never charged for Becky's murder. In 1962, this same man was convicted and sentenced to death for the 1961 beating death of 13-year old Jane Benfield of Centralia, Columbia County. His death sentence was later commuted to life. He died in prison of cancer in the '90s. Rebecca Ann Triska was declared dead in 1967. Her date of death was recorded as September 19, 1965, seven years after she was last seen. Her body has never been found.

clothingAndAccessoriesArticles.description: Green cashmere sweater; black velvet slacks

circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance:
Gerald was 15 years old when disappeared without trace as well as his good friend Robert Rasmussen. It was the 9th September 1957. They had just vanished. Their clothes were found on the Flat Rock, near the Plymouth Harbour.
For three days they were searched without result. Theories from law enforcement were from they were drowned to that their disappearance was a hoax. Nothing could be verified but the uncertainty was unbearable. 
Bodies were not found nor nobody seen them anymore.

physicalDescription.eyeDescription: Gerald looked older than 15 at that time.

Anna Kenneway was married on 08/23/42.  This was Anna Kenneway's 2nd marriage and her new husband's 2nd or 3rd marriage.  By all accounts the marriage appeared to be in turmoil with constant arguing.  Anna Kenneway disappeared under suspicious circumstances in June 1954.  She never made any contact with her children, relatives or friends.  

While her children from her first marriage contacted the police regarding her disappearance, a report was not taken due to the fact that at that time the police required the husband to file the report, which he never did.  Persistence by her children eventually lead to a cursory police investigation on 07/20/54.  That police investigation has since been purged.  

The case was re-opened on 06/01/2006: Manchester Police Case M2006-08001, NCIC #M361498980, Message #3230148, ORI #CT0007711.

notes.description: Granddaughter still actively involved in locating and/or discovering whatever happened to her grandmother.

Annibel went to work at Walgreens in Syracuse NY on Friday 4-10-1952 and never returned.

Thomas "Tommy" Richey left home to go fishing on the Rogue River on July 11, 1950. He liked to fish in an area where there was a cable car above the river that you could move to the middle and fish from above. His fishing pole as well as several fish he caught were found inside the cable car. He has not been seen or heard from since that day. The cable car was braked in the middle of the river which is why it was presumed something happened causing him to fall or jump and ultimately drown. The cable car and fishing hole are located 4 miles outside Prospect, Oregon on Mill Creek Lane and 6 miles upriver from the Lost Creek Dam.

images.caption: Portrait of Tommy Richey
Thomas Richey age 16

Thomas E Burns was convicted of murdering his common-in-law wife, Marie Coleman on January 10, 1959.  He allegedly boasted that he had cut up her body and disposed of it. This was the first murder case in PA to be tried without a body.  Her remains have never been found.

15 month old Vilate Young disappeared from a large family picnic on the 4th of July at the Newell Steed ranch, near Bryce Canyon, Utah. She was watching one of her relatives churn ice cream near one of the farm's outbuildings when she walked away. Airplanes and bloodhounds attempted to track the little girl, but she was never found.
Aletha was last seen on 12/17/1955 in Douglas County, Colorado.
Carl Moody was last known to be in San Francisco, California on October 23, 1951.

On June 18, 1950, Barbara Joe Kelley’s boyfriend Henry Baird was located deceased on the beach at Table Bluff. Kelley’s belonging were located nearby Baird’s remains. Law enforcement and family has been unable to contact Kelley and she remains missing today.

Thomas "Tommy" Novak was last seen about 10 pm on July 25th, 1958 on the Lawrence Carter Ranch in Park Valley, Utah wearing a tan shirt and blue jeans. No cars or horses were reported missing, so it is assumed the boy left on foot. He did not take any extra clothing or food with him. At first it was thought the boy may be headed to Finley, Nevada or Idaho. A boy resembling Tommy had been seen trying to broad a train at Brigham City.

On Aug 4th, a boy in a tan shirt, blue slacks, a western style straw hat, carrying an old type .22 and a bundle of cattails was seen at the Ogden stockyard and later the rail yards. The boy told a railroad
checker that he was going to Wells, Nevada. The last possible sighting of Tommy came from a rancher in Snowville, Utah who reported that he saw the boy on the evening of Aug 11th. The Box Elder County Sheriff's Office, led by Sheriff Warren Hyde, and many locals on horseback conducted extensive searches of the rugged area around Park Valley but did not find any trace of the missing boy. Railroad authorities as far as California were notified to be on the look out for Tommy riding the rails. 

Before moving to Utah about a year prior, the family had lived in Nevada. The authorities from Nevada were familiar with Tommy and noted that the boy had an interest in trucks. He was known to hitch
rides or stow-away in large trucks. Tommy had one sibling, a four-year-old brother, at the time of his disappearance.

notes.description: Tommy was not reported missing until until July 29th since he had run away from home several times before but never stayed away too long.

clothingAndAccessoriesArticles.description: Last seen wearing a tan shirt and blue jeans; possibly a western style straw hat, an old type .22 rifle, and a bundle of cattails.

circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance: On 06/26/1953, at approximately 9:30 p.m., William G. Dolan, age 27, attempted to swim across the 300-foot channel that connects Sodus Bay to Lake Ontario.  Witnesses observed Dolan disappear under the water in Sodus Bay, approximately halfway across the channel and not resurface.  An extensive search in the following days for Dolan by the US Coast Guard, New York State Police and Sodus Point Fire Department had negative results.  Dolan was an Airman First Class in the United States Air Force at the time, assigned to the Sampson Air Force Base.

investigatingAgencies.name: New York State Police - Troop E Headquarters

agency.name: New York State Police - Troop E Headquarters

primaryInvestigatingAgency.name: New York State Police - Troop E Headquarters

circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance: Emily Williams was last known to be in Del Norte County in 1957.

On July 7, 1958 Mildred's divorce was granted against her husband. That day after court, Mildred had contact with a family member. Later that evening Mildred was supposed to meet another person (their identity is not known). Mildred has not been seen or heard of since July 7, 1958.

clothingAndAccessoriesArticles.description: Last see wearing a white and orchid (purplish and blueish dress colored) dress

circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance: Clara was last known to be living in Cleveland, Ohio between 1950 and 1952.

circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance: Phillip was last seen leaving Oklahoma City and heading towards Dallas, Tx.

circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance: On January 31, 1950, Ruth Jock was reported missing. Ruth was last seen on January 29, 1950 at about 7:30 am. She has not been seen or heard from since.

investigatingAgencies.name: New York State Police - Troop B

circumstances.circumstancesOfDisappearance:
On July 31, 1955 (According to newspaper reports), five individuals from Salt Lake City rented a small aluminum boat from Moon Lake Lodge/Resort, Duchesne County, Utah. Witnesses said the boat had two motors: one at the back and one on the side. The sheriff stated the group was too large for the small boat. Calvin, Ruby, and Clarence were on that boat. Witnesses said both motors were running, and the boat was swinging in circles before it overturned. The boat sank immediately. The sheriff stated that the weight of the two motors took it to the bottom despite air compartments in the aluminum craft. The accident occurred in the deepest part of Moon Lake, estimated to be 115 to 125 feet deep.  Calvin Wayne Bosen (MP127057), Ruby Mae Simons Bosen (MP127055), and Clarence Delmer Bosen (MP127053) drowned.

A full-scale search operation was conducted by the Duchesne County Sheriff’s Office. A large search was conducted with a group using grappling hooks. Four boats dragged the lake in a half-mile area. Sheriff Stevenson requested a diver but could not locate one who could handle the depth until Tuesday, Aug 2nd. The sheriff used a light aircraft to fly over the lake, looking for the bodies or the boat. Areas of the lake where debris is noted to wash ashore were also checked. Debris was also checked in the spring months of the next year, and a report from 1957 also states that no
remains were ever found.

Biocca was last seen on January 20, 1950, after leaving his house to take a morning walk.
